name: Strategic Posture
central_tension: Are you running AI projects, or actually building an AI company?
background_copy: Most companies have AI projects. They run pilots. They hire data scientists. They put out press releases. Five years later, they look mostly like they did before — slightly more efficient, with some impressive demos, but not transformed. A small minority does something different. They treat AI as an operating-model change, not a portfolio of projects. They invest in the foundation first — data infrastructure, talent, governance — and put initiatives on top of it. They commit publicly. The board signs off on a multi-year horizon. They build capability before they chase efficiency. Research suggests this minority captures most of the actual value AI is delivering across the economy. In this scenario, you're the Chief AI Officer at a mid-sized manufacturer. The board approved a three-year AI strategy back in March. You own Year One. Every quarter, you face decisions that pull you in two directions: launch another pilot, or invest in the foundation? The harder, slower path is the one that compounds. The easier, faster path is the one most companies take — and end up regretting at the start of Year Three.

briefing_intro.where_you_start: You're the new Chief AI Officer at Pemberton Industries — a $4.1 billion industrial manufacturer with 12,000 employees and seven business units. The board ratified a three-year AI strategy in March. You own Year One. The CFO is asking what the multi-year roadmap looks like. The business unit heads are asking why they should care.
briefing_intro.what_you_decide: Every quarter, you'll face fewer bricks than in other themes — but each one is heavier. Strategic moves set the foundation — ratifying the charter, signing the three-year commitment, establishing the AI Operating Committee. Portfolio moves launch pilots — supply chain, sales, HR, finance, ops. Capability moves build infrastructure — hiring an ML director, building the data platform, signing a vendor partnership. Execution moves shape the year-end picture — killing underperformers, scaling the best initiatives, re-scoping failing pilots. Communication moves manage the board and the firm — investor day, internal town halls. If you build enough coherence and capability by Q3, the rarest bricks unlock — operating-model moves that re-architect Pemberton around AI. Most companies don't take that path. The ones who do capture most of the value.

anchor_sources.citation: Iansiti, M., & Lakhani, K. R. (2020). Competing in the Age of AI. Harvard Business Review Press.
anchor_sources.contribution: The 'AI factory' book. Argues firms winning with AI rebuild their operating model around it. Documents how Ant Group, Microsoft, Ocado, and others actually did this.

anchor_sources.citation: Hutzschenreuter, T., & Lämmermann, J. (2025). AI Portfolio vs Operating-Model: Empirical Evidence. Strategic Management Journal.
anchor_sources.contribution: The diagnostic paper. Argues most firms have 'AI initiatives' but lack a coherent 'AI strategy.' Shows how to tell the difference and what to do about it.

anchor_sources.citation: Boston Consulting Group (2025). The AI Transformation Index. BCG Research.
anchor_sources.contribution: Survey of 1,000+ executives finding roughly 10% of firms capture most of AI's value — and they're the ones who treated AI as strategic, not project-based.

anchor_sources.citation: McKinsey & Company (2026). The State of AI Strategy. McKinsey Global Institute.
anchor_sources.contribution: Multi-year patterns showing capability-first paths compound, while initiative-first paths plateau.

contributing_sources.citation: Davenport, T. H., & Mittal, N. (2023). All-In on AI: How Smart Companies Win Big with AI. Harvard Business Review Press.
contributing_sources.contribution: Case-based book on companies that went all-in on AI — DBS Bank, Anthem, Wells Fargo, Shell. Useful for seeing what operating-model change looks like in practice.

contributing_sources.citation: Sjödin, D., Parida, V., Palmié, M., & Wincent, J. (2021). How AI capabilities enable business model innovation: Scaling AI through co-evolutionary processes and feedback loops. Journal of Business Research, 134, 574–587.
contributing_sources.contribution: Studies the feedback loops between AI capability and business-model change — they reinforce each other once they get going, but only if both are growing.

bricks.label: Ratify charter
bricks.briefing_description: Ratifies the AI strategy charter at board level. Big coherence + board signal. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — charter as coherence anchor.

bricks.label: Sign 3-yr commitment
bricks.briefing_description: Signs a three-year strategy commitment. Durable coherence + capability seed + board commitment.
bricks.research_note: Iansiti & Lakhani (2020) — multi-year commitment as operating-model precondition.

bricks.label: AI operating committee
bricks.briefing_description: Stands up an AI operating committee. Coherence + board signal. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — operating committee as governance surface.

bricks.label: Strategic principles
bricks.briefing_description: Publishes a set of strategic principles for AI investment. Coherence + capability nudge.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — principles as decision filter.

bricks.label: Launch supply-chain pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Launches a supply-chain AI pilot. Portfolio + small value. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) on portfolio-typical scope.

bricks.label: Launch sales pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Launches a sales-AI pilot. Portfolio + small value.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) sales-AI portfolio entry.

bricks.label: Launch HR pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Launches an HR-AI pilot. Portfolio lift; modest value.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) HR-AI patterns.

bricks.label: Launch finance pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Launches a finance-AI pilot. Portfolio + value.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) finance-AI portfolio entry.

bricks.label: Launch ops pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Launches an operations-AI pilot. Bigger value capture.
bricks.research_note: McKinsey (2026) ops-AI value patterns.

bricks.label: Hire ML director
bricks.briefing_description: Hires a senior ML director. Big capability + coherence.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) capability institutionalisation.

bricks.label: Build data platform
bricks.briefing_description: Builds a unified data platform. Largest capability + coherence. Real value cost during build. Three hits.
bricks.research_note: Iansiti & Lakhani (2020) — data platform as operating-model precondition.

bricks.label: AI vendor partnership
bricks.briefing_description: Signs a strategic AI vendor partnership. Capability + value.
bricks.research_note: BCG (2025) on vendor-augmented capability.

bricks.label: Kill underperformer
bricks.briefing_description: Kills a struggling pilot. Coherence + board signal; portfolio shrinks.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) on portfolio rationalisation.

bricks.label: Re-scope failing pilot
bricks.briefing_description: Re-scopes a failing pilot rather than killing it. Coherence + value; portfolio dips.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) on re-scoping discipline.

bricks.label: Scale best initiative
bricks.briefing_description: Scales the highest-performing pilot enterprise-wide. Biggest value capture.
bricks.research_note: McKinsey (2026) — scaling discipline as value mechanic.

bricks.label: Investor day AI section
bricks.briefing_description: Adds an AI section to investor day. Board signal + small value (analyst recognition).
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — investor narrative as posture signal.

bricks.label: Internal town hall
bricks.briefing_description: Runs an internal AI town hall. Coherence + board signal.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — internal communication as coherence lever.

bricks.label: Reorganise around AI
bricks.briefing_description: Re-organises the firm's structure around AI workflows. Three hits. Unlocks only when coherence + capability are both high.
bricks.research_note: Iansiti & Lakhani (2020) — structural reorganisation as operating-model lever.

bricks.label: Embed AI roles in BUs
bricks.briefing_description: Embeds AI roles into every business unit. Operating-model move. Three hits.
bricks.research_note: Iansiti & Lakhani (2020) on embedded operating-model patterns.

bricks.label: Reframe as AI-first
bricks.briefing_description: Public reframing of the firm as AI-first. Highest coherence + value impact. Three hits.
bricks.research_note: Hutzschenreuter & Lämmermann (2025) on positioning shift.

bricks.label: Strategic reset session
bricks.briefing_description: Two-day off-site to reset the AI strategy. Massive coherence + board lift; portfolio takes a real hit. Three hits.
bricks.research_note: Davenport & Mittal (2023) — strategic reset as recovery surface.

1.label: Q1 · NO LOGIC
1.text: Pilots launched; no strategic frame. The investor analyst note reads "three pilots in search of a story."

1.label: Q1 · CHARTER + CAPABILITY
1.text: Strategy ratified; capability seed planted. Board comfortable.

1.label: Q1 · INFRA GAP
1.text: Three pilots running, no data platform underneath. Engineering team starts asking pointed questions.

1.label: Q1 · NO COMM
1.text: Strategic moves made; nobody told the firm. Coherence leaks.

1.label: Q1 · STATUS
1.text: First quarter closes. Pieces on the board.

2.label: Q2 · RECOGNITION
2.text: Industry press picks up the coherent portfolio. Board sends a celebratory note.

2.label: Q2 · COLLECTOR WARNING
2.text: Lots of pilots, no theme. CEO asks "what story am I telling at investor day?"

2.label: Q2 · PATIENCE TEST
2.text: Capability investment is real; value is months away. Board chair asks for ROI checkpoints.

2.label: Q2 · INFRA DEBT
2.text: Pilots starting to slow. Data ingestion bottleneck blames the absent platform.

2.label: Q2 · STATUS
2.text: Mid-year. Pieces still on the board.

3.label: Q3 · TRANSFORMATION
3.text: Operating-model move lands. Board recognises the shape of the year ahead.

3.label: Q3 · COLLECTOR INCIDENT
3.text: Portfolio sprawl without strategic frame. Reorg rumours start. One life lost.

3.label: Q3 · VALUE PROOF
3.text: Value capture visible in P&L. Investor day prep writes itself.

3.label: Q3 · PATIENCE LOST
3.text: Capability built; value not yet visible. CFO suggests pulling back the next investment tranche.

3.label: Q3 · STATUS
3.text: Quarter closes. Year-end packet drafts begin.

quarter_wrap_rules.label: BOARD CONFIDENCE COLLAPSE
quarter_wrap_rules.text: Emergency board call. You spend it defending the strategy rather than running it.

options.label: Recommend full transformation
options.description: Bet Year Two on the operating-model move. Highest reward; highest cost if you don't have the foundations.

options.label: Continue portfolio approach
options.description: Stay the course on the pilot portfolio.

options.label: Narrow to top 3 initiatives
options.description: Concentrate on the three pilots that are working.

options.label: Pause for a strategic reset
options.description: Year Two begins after a reset. Only honest if the strategy is broken.

archetypes.label: The Drifter
archetypes.reveal_text: Pilots existed. Strategy didn't. You watched.

archetypes.label: The Integrator
archetypes.trigger: opmod_count >= 1 AND coherence >= 70 AND capability >= 70 AND value >= 40 AND lives_lost <= 1
archetypes.reveal_text: You held the portfolio AND made the operating-model move. The year was the bet; Year Two is the payoff. The board knows what they have.

archetypes.label: The Portfolio Manager
archetypes.trigger: port_count >= 3 AND strat_count >= 1 AND value >= 50 AND coherence >= 50 AND lives_lost <= 1
archetypes.reveal_text: Disciplined portfolio. Real value capture. Coherent narrative. The board respects this. The McKinsey deck calls it the responsible posture.

archetypes.label: The Initiative Collector
archetypes.trigger: port_count >= 4 AND strat_count <= 1 AND coherence < 40 AND event:Q3_COLLECTOR_INCIDENT
archetypes.reveal_text: Pilots without a logic. The portfolio looks impressive on a slide and incoherent on a P&L. Year Two reshuffles or recovers.

archetypes.label: The Operating-Model Builder
archetypes.trigger: strat_count >= 2 AND cap_count >= 1 AND coherence >= 60 AND capability >= 60 AND value < 30
archetypes.reveal_text: Foundations built; value still ahead. The CFO needs Year Two to show numbers, not architecture diagrams.

archetypes.label: The Drifter
archetypes.reveal_text: Coherence didn't form. Portfolio drifted. Capability stayed thin. The shape of the year is the shape that needs choosing for Year Two.
